response: The internet began in the 1960s with US Department of Defense networking research, and ARPANET was first demonstrated in 1969 with a message sent from UCLA to Stanford Research Institute. A major turning point came in 1983 with the adoption of TCP/IP, while Tim Berners-Lee’s invention of the World Wide Web in 1989 and the release of Mosaic in 1993 made the internet far more usable for the public. The text argues that the internet evolved through key phases—including the dot-com boom and 2001 crash, the rise of social media in the mid-2000s, smartphones after the iPhone’s 2007 launch, and cloud computing with AWS in 2006—into essential global infrastructure. Today, it connects over 5 billion people and supports nearly every part of modern life.
